fe eee ate ath te ote Sar get wet bi G9 5 ae GEG gle GS ES Sl

“O Allah, as for he who is entrusted with an affair of my Nation and who is hard upon them, then be hard upon him; and as for he who is entrusted with an affair of my Nation and who is then kind and gentle with them, then be kind and gentle with him."

An-Nawawee said, “This is from the severest of warnings about being hard upon people; yet at the same, it is from the strongest of encouragements about being gentle and kind with them. And there are many other Ahadeeth that convey a similar meaning.”

Whether they realize it or not, some Imams - may Allah guide them to what is right - are hard upon the people. They might delay the Iqaamah, which holds people back from work and from

"I Related by Muslim (1828) and Ahmad (24101).

other duties they must fulfill, Made to wait longer than necessary, a worshipper might face the difficult question of whether he may pray alone or whether he should wait for the Imam. All of this hardship is unnecessary and reflects poorly on the Imam. The wise Imam is one who sets a specific, known time for each prayer, so that if he is late for some unexpected reason, those present can begin the prayer. This is just one of the ways that an Imam shows kindness and consideration to the congregation of his Masjid. And it is Allah 4 who grants success.

9) The Permissibility Of Lying Down in The Masjid

As is indicated by the Prophet's practice, it is permissible to lie down in the Masjid. ‘Abduliah Ibn Zaid Ibn ‘Aasim # reported that he once saw the Messenger of Allah #% lying down in the Masjid, with one of his legs placed over the other. Ibn Shihaab related that Sa’eed Ibn Al-Musayyib said, “Umar and ‘Uthmaan a would do that as well.” If you do lie down in the Masjid, you must be sure that no private area of your body will become exposed as a result, for depending on what kind of clothes you are wearing, you may expose a private area of your body if you place one leg over the other.

Related Issue: In an attempt to stay away from any act of irreverence in the Masjid, some people avoid stretching out their legs in the direct of the Qiblah. Yet there is no irreverence in doing so; therefore, whether he is inside of the Masjid or not, a person who stretches out his legs in the direction of the Qiblah is not sinning. Another Related Issue: When you are in the Masjid, if you do stretch your legs out in the direction of the Qiblah, your legs must not be pointing towards copies of the Qur’an.?) You must show good manners and reverence to Allah’s Speech. In gatherings, it "l Related by Bukhaaree (475), Muslim (2100), At-Tirmidhee (2765), AnNassaaee (721), Abu Daawood (4866), Ahmad (15995), Maalik (417), and Ad-Daarimee (2656). 2) In most Masjids, Qur’ans are placed before the worshippers, at the front o} the Masjid.

Chapter - 11 The Manners Of f Attending Masjids_ 259

is common for people to reproach someone for stretching his legs out towards them; imagine, then, the greater disrespect shown when one points his legs towards the Quran.

10) The Permissibility Of Sleeping In The Masjid

If you feel the need to do so, you may sleep in the Masjid. The People of Suffah''! would sleep in the Masjid, and, as Naafai’ related, before getting married, Ibn ‘Umar \# would also sleep in the Masjid.?!

Related Issue: If one sleeps in the Masjid and has a wet dream, he should hurry to leave as soon as he wakes up in order to take a shower and enter into a state of purity.)

11) The Prohibition Of Buying And Selling In Masjids

It is forbidden to buy and sell in the Masjid. A Masjid is not built to be a store or marketplace; instead it is built for the remembrance of Allah, for establishing prayer, for educating Muslims about their religious affairs, and so on. In fact, if you see aman buying or selling anything in the Masjid, you should make a supplication against him, saying, “May Allah not make your trade profitable.” Abu Hurairah 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah #2 said,

“If you see someone buy or sell in the Masjid, then say, ‘May Allah not make your trade profitable.’...’"4]

" The People of Suffah: Poor Companions 4 who lived in the Masjid of the Messenger of AJlah #8; there they ate and slept.

¥) Bukhaaree related this narration (442).

‘41 Related by At-Tirmidhee (1321), who said, “This hadith is Hasan Ghareeb.”” Because of the grading of the hadith, scholars have differed about its application. Some scholars, such as Ahmad and Ishaq, have

Related Issue: Regarding the ruling for buying and selling in rooms or halls that are connected to the Masjid, or halls that are used for prayer, the Permanent Council (of Scholars in Saudi Arabia) issued this ruling: “If a room is specifically used for prayer, and if it is connected to the Masjid, it is not permissible to buy or sell in it, nor is it permissible to advertise products inside of it. The Prophet 2% indeed said,

‘If you see someone buy or sell in the Masjid, then say: May Allah not make your trade profitable...”

As for rooms, then we need to elaborate. If a room is within the enclosure of a Masjid, then it takes the ruling of a Masjid, and what we said about halls applies to that room. But if the room is outside the Masjid’s enclosure, it does not take the ruling of the Masjid, even if there is a door that connects it to the Masjid. This tuling is based on the fact that the Prophet's house, in which ‘Aaisha % lived, had a door that was connected to the Masjid, yet his house did not take the same ruling as the Masjid.”

Question: In saying, “May Allah not make your trade profitable,” should we distinguish between a person who knows the ruling for trading in the Masjid and a person who doesn’t know the ruling?

Answer: The plain meaning of the hadith’s wording indicates that we should say the phrase both to a person who knows the ruling for trading in the Masjid and to a person who doesn't know the ruling.

12) The Prohibition Of Asking Or Making An Announcement In The - Masjid About A Lost Possession

Allah’s Masjids are built for His remembrance, His glorification, the recitation of the Qur’an, and prayer; they are not built as

ruled that it is Makrooh (disliked) to buy and sell in the Masjid. Other scholars have ruled that it is permissible to buy and sell in the Masjid. And the hadith is also related by Ad-Daarimee (1401).

Chapter - 11 The Manners Of Attending Masjids 261

places where people go to ask about their lost possessions. Abu Hurairah % related that the Messenger of Allah #¢ said,

56 Grle 635 V YB ae p Lt ES Gee yn

“Whoever hears a person ask or make an announcement in the Masjid about a lost possession, should say, ‘May Allah not return it to you,' for Masjids were not built for this,"

Therefore, upon seeing a person who is asking about a lost possession, you should say to him, ‘May Allah not return it to you.”

13) Speaking Loudly In The Masjid

While he was in the Masjid, Ka’ab Ibn Maalik asked Ibn Abee Hadrad to pay him back for a loan. In the ensuing discussion, they raised their voices, to the degree that the Messenger of Allah # heard them, even though he was in his home. He # went out to them, uncovering the barrier from his apartment. He # then

called out, “O Ka’ab.” Ka’ab # said, ‘Here I am, answering your call, O Messenger of Allah #%.”” He #& said,

“Remove (forgive) a part of this debt of yours,” after which he signaled that Ka’ab should forgive Ibn Abee Hadrad % for half of the debt. Ka’ab 4 said, “I have indeed

done so, O Messenger of Allah.” The Prophet # then said to Ibn Abee Hadrad #,

“Stand and pay him back,’?)

') The hadith is related with different wordings by Muslim (568), Ahmad (8382, 9161), At-Tirmidhee (1321), Abu Daawood (473), Ibn Maajah (767), and Ad-Daarimee (1401).

2) Related by Bukhaaree (457), Muslim (1558), Ahmad (15364), An-Nassaaee (408), Abu Daawood (3595), Ibn Maajah (2429), and Ad-Daarimee (2587).

Upon contemplating the two above-mentioned Altadeeth, one might conclude that they are in apparent contradiction with one another. The Messenger of Allah 2% did not reproach those who raised their voices in the Masjid; instead, he simply told Ka’ab 4, one of the two who raised their voices, to reduce the debt by one half. The Prophet #% never delayed issuing a ruling after its necessary time; here the Prophet ## had the opportunity to reproach them for raising their voices, yet he didn’t do so, which indicates that raising one’s voice in the Masjid is not forbidden. “‘Umar’s narration, on the other hand, indicates that it is Makrooh (disliked) to raise one’s voice in the Masjid. Possessing a noble character, ‘Umar . would not have reproached them had he not known some proof from the Prophet #%; hence his reproach holds the same weight as a hadith that is ascribed to the Prophet #8. Perhaps the combined understanding of the two narrations can be explained by a saying of Maalik (in one of his two narrations): “A distinction must be made between one raising his voice for [imparting] knowledge, for [some] good [purpose], or for something that is necessary - which is all permissible - and between one raising his voice with clamor, [nonsense], and whatever is similar - which is all forbidden.)

Related Issue: The Permanent Council (of Scholars in Saudi Arabia) issued this ruling about begging for money: Unless one needs to do so, it is forbidden to beg for money, a ruling that "I Related by Bukhaaree (470).

fe eee ate ath te ote Sar get wet bi G9 5 ae GEG gle GS ES Sl

"Ya Allah, adapun orang yang diamanahkan urusan umatku dan ia berlaku keras terhadap mereka, maka keraskanlah ia; dan adapun orang yang diamanahkan urusan umatku dan ia berlaku lembut dan baik kepada mereka, maka berlaku lembutlah dan baikilah ia."

An-Nawawee berkata, "Ini termasuk peringatan paling keras tentang berlaku keras kepada orang-orang; namun pada saat yang sama, ini juga termasuk dorongan paling kuat untuk berlaku lembut dan baik kepada mereka. Dan masih banyak hadith lain yang menyampaikan makna serupa."

Disadari atau tidak, sebagian Imam — semoga Allah membimbing mereka kepada kebenaran — berlaku keras kepada orang-orang. Mereka mungkin menunda iqamah, yang menahan orang dari pekerjaan dan dari

"I Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (1828) dan Ahmad (24101).

kewajiban-kewajiban lain yang harus mereka tunaikan. Dipaksa menunggu lebih lama dari yang diperlukan, seorang jamaah mungkin menghadapi pertanyaan sulit apakah ia boleh shalat sendirian atau harus menunggu Imam. Semua kesulitan ini tidak perlu dan mencerminkan buruknya sifat Imam. Imam yang bijaksana adalah yang menetapkan waktu tertentu dan diketahui untuk setiap shalat, sehingga jika ia terlambat karena suatu alasan yang tidak terduga, mereka yang hadir dapat memulai shalat. Inilah salah satu cara Imam menunjukkan kebaikan dan perhatian kepada jamaah masjidnya. Dan Allah-lah yang memberikan taufik.

9) Kebolehan Berbaring di Dalam Masjid

Sebagaimana ditunjukkan oleh praktik Nabi, diperbolehkan berbaring di dalam masjid. 'Abdullah Ibn Zaid Ibn 'Aasim radhiyallahu 'anhu melaporkan bahwa ia pernah melihat Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam berbaring di masjid dengan salah satu kakinya diletakkan di atas kaki yang lain. Ibn Shihaab meriwayatkan bahwa Sa'eed Ibn Al-Musayyib berkata, "'Umar dan 'Utsman radhiyallahu 'anhuma juga melakukan hal yang sama." Jika Anda berbaring di masjid, Anda harus memastikan bahwa tidak ada aurat tubuh Anda yang akan terlihat sebagai akibatnya, karena tergantung pakaian yang Anda kenakan, Anda mungkin memperlihatkan aurat jika meletakkan satu kaki di atas kaki yang lain.

Masalah Terkait: Dalam upaya menghindari segala bentuk kurang adab di masjid, sebagian orang menghindari meluruskan kaki mereka ke arah kiblat. Namun tidak ada ketidaksopanan dalam hal itu; oleh karena itu, baik di dalam masjid maupun tidak, seseorang yang meluruskan kakinya ke arah kiblat tidaklah berdosa. Masalah Terkait Lainnya: Ketika Anda berada di masjid, jika Anda meluruskan kaki ke arah kiblat, kaki Anda tidak boleh mengarah ke mushaf Al-Qur'an. Anda harus menunjukkan adab yang baik dan penghormatan terhadap Kalam Allah. Dalam perkumpulan, 1) Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (475), Muslim (2100), At-Tirmidhee (2765), An-Nassaaee (721), Abu Daawood (4866), Ahmad (15995), Maalik (417), dan Ad-Daarimee (2656). 2) Di sebagian besar masjid, Al-Qur'an diletakkan di hadapan jamaah, di bagian depan masjid.

Bab - 11 Adab-Adab Menghadiri Masjid_ 259

sudah lazim orang menegur seseorang yang meluruskan kakinya ke arah mereka; bayangkan, betapa lebih tidak hormatnya seseorang yang mengarahkan kakinya ke arah Al-Qur'an.

10) Kebolehan Tidur di Dalam Masjid

Jika Anda merasa perlu melakukannya, Anda boleh tidur di masjid. Ahlus Shuffah tidur di masjid, dan sebagaimana yang diriwayatkan oleh Naafi', sebelum menikah, Ibn 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu juga tidur di masjid.

Masalah Terkait: Jika seseorang tidur di masjid dan mengalami mimpi basah, ia harus segera pergi begitu ia terbangun untuk mandi dan bersuci.

11) Larangan Jual Beli di Masjid

Diharamkan melakukan jual beli di masjid. Masjid tidak dibangun untuk menjadi toko atau pasar; melainkan dibangun untuk dzikir kepada Allah, mendirikan shalat, mendidik kaum Muslimin tentang urusan agama mereka, dan sebagainya. Bahkan, jika Anda melihat seseorang membeli atau menjual sesuatu di masjid, Anda hendaknya berdoa buruk untuknya, dengan mengatakan, "Semoga Allah tidak memberkahi perdaganganmu." Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda,

"Jika kalian melihat seseorang membeli atau menjual di masjid, maka katakanlah, 'Semoga Allah tidak memberkahi perdaganganmu.'...'"

" Ahlus Shuffah: Para Sahabat yang miskin yang tinggal di masjid Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam; di sanalah mereka makan dan tidur.

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (442).

Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (1321), yang berkata, "Hadith ini Hasan Ghareeb." Karena derajat hadith ini, para ulama berselisih pendapat tentang penerapannya. Sebagian ulama, seperti Ahmad dan Ishaq, telah

Masalah Terkait: Mengenai hukum jual beli di ruangan atau aula yang terhubung dengan masjid, atau aula yang digunakan untuk shalat, Majelis Ulama Tetap (di Arab Saudi) mengeluarkan fatwa berikut: "Jika sebuah ruangan khusus digunakan untuk shalat, dan terhubung dengan masjid, tidak diperbolehkan melakukan jual beli di dalamnya, dan tidak pula diperbolehkan mengiklankan produk di dalamnya. Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam memang bersabda,

'Jika kalian melihat seseorang membeli atau menjual di masjid, maka katakanlah: Semoga Allah tidak memberkahi perdaganganmu...'

Adapun ruangan-ruangan, maka kita perlu menjelaskan lebih lanjut. Jika sebuah ruangan berada di dalam lingkungan masjid, maka ia mengambil hukum masjid, dan apa yang kami katakan tentang aula berlaku pula untuk ruangan tersebut. Namun jika ruangan itu berada di luar lingkungan masjid, ia tidak mengambil hukum masjid, meskipun ada pintu yang menghubungkannya dengan masjid. Hukum ini didasarkan pada fakta bahwa rumah Nabi, yang ditempati oleh 'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha, memiliki pintu yang terhubung dengan masjid, namun rumahnya tidak mengambil hukum yang sama dengan masjid."

Pertanyaan: Dalam mengucapkan, "Semoga Allah tidak memberkahi perdaganganmu," apakah kita harus membedakan antara orang yang mengetahui hukum berdagang di masjid dan orang yang tidak mengetahui hukumnya?

Jawaban: Makna zahir dari lafaz hadith mengindikasikan bahwa kita hendaknya mengucapkan kalimat tersebut baik kepada orang yang mengetahui hukum berdagang di masjid maupun yang tidak mengetahuinya.

12) Larangan Meminta atau Mengumumkan di Masjid tentang Barang yang Hilang

Masjid-masjid Allah dibangun untuk dzikirnya, pengagungan-Nya, pembacaan Al-Qur'an, dan shalat; bukan dibangun sebagai

berpendapat bahwa hukumnya makruh (tidak disukai) untuk jual beli di masjid. Ulama lain berpendapat bahwa diperbolehkan jual beli di masjid. Dan hadith tersebut juga diriwayatkan oleh Ad-Daarimee (1401).

Bab - 11 Adab-Adab Menghadiri Masjid 261

tempat orang-orang pergi untuk bertanya tentang barang mereka yang hilang. Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda,

56 Grle 635 V YB ae p Lt ES Gee yn

"Barangsiapa mendengar seseorang bertanya atau mengumumkan di masjid tentang barang yang hilang, hendaklah ia berkata, 'Semoga Allah tidak mengembalikannya kepadamu,' karena masjid bukan dibangun untuk ini."

Oleh karena itu, ketika melihat seseorang yang sedang mencari barang yang hilang, Anda hendaknya berkata kepadanya, 'Semoga Allah tidak mengembalikannya kepadamu.'

13) Berbicara Keras di Masjid

Ketika berada di masjid, Ka'ab Ibn Maalik meminta Ibn Abee Hadrad untuk membayar kembali pinjaman kepadanya. Dalam diskusi yang terjadi, keduanya meninggikan suara mereka, hingga Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam mendengar mereka, meskipun beliau sedang berada di rumahnya. Beliau keluar menemui mereka, menyingkap tirai dari kamarnya. Beliau kemudian

memanggil, "Wahai Ka'ab." Ka'ab radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, 'Aku di sini, memenuhi panggilanmu, wahai Rasulullah.' Beliau bersabda,

"Hapuskanlah (maafkanlah) sebagian dari utangmu ini," kemudian beliau memberi isyarat agar Ka'ab memaafkan Ibn Abee Hadrad radhiyallahu 'anhu untuk setengah dari utang tersebut. Ka'ab radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Sungguh aku telah

melakukannya, wahai Rasulullah." Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam kemudian berkata kepada Ibn Abee Hadrad radhiyallahu 'anhu,

"Berdirilah dan bayarlah kepadanya."

1) Hadith ini diriwayatkan dengan redaksi yang berbeda oleh Muslim (568), Ahmad (8382, 9161), At-Tirmidhee (1321), Abu Daawood (473), Ibn Maajah (767), dan Ad-Daarimee (1401).

2) Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (457), Muslim (1558), Ahmad (15364), An-Nassaaee (408), Abu Daawood (3595), Ibn Maajah (2429), dan Ad-Daarimee (2587).

Ketika merenungkan dua hadith yang disebutkan di atas, seseorang mungkin menyimpulkan bahwa keduanya tampaknya saling bertentangan. Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam tidak menegur mereka yang meninggikan suara di masjid; sebaliknya, beliau hanya memberitahu Ka'ab radhiyallahu 'anhu, salah satu dari keduanya yang meninggikan suara, untuk mengurangi utang sebesar setengahnya. Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam tidak pernah menunda pengeluaran hukum setelah waktu yang diperlukan; di sini Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam memiliki kesempatan untuk menegur mereka karena meninggikan suara, namun beliau tidak melakukannya, yang mengindikasikan bahwa meninggikan suara di masjid tidaklah diharamkan. Riwayat 'Umar, di sisi lain, mengindikasikan bahwa makruh meninggikan suara di masjid. Memiliki akhlak yang mulia, 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu tidak akan menegur mereka jika ia tidak mengetahui suatu dalil dari Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam; karenanya tegurannya memiliki bobot yang sama dengan hadith yang disandarkan kepada Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Mungkin pemahaman gabungan dari kedua riwayat tersebut dapat dijelaskan dengan perkataan Maalik (dalam salah satu dari dua riwayatnya): "Harus dibedakan antara meninggikan suara untuk [menyampaikan] ilmu, untuk [suatu] kebaikan, atau untuk sesuatu yang diperlukan — yang semuanya diperbolehkan — dan antara meninggikan suara dengan keributan, [omong kosong], dan yang serupa — yang semuanya diharamkan."

Masalah Terkait: Dewan Tetap (Ulama di Arab Saudi) mengeluarkan fatwa ini tentang meminta-minta: Kecuali seseorang benar-benar membutuhkannya, hukum meminta-minta adalah haram, suatu fatwa yang "Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (470).

A المسجد is not built to be a store or marketplace; instead it is built for the الذكر of الله, for establishing الصلاة, for educating المسلمون about their religious affairs, and لذا on. In fact, إذا you see aman buying or selling anything in the المسجد, you should make a الدعاء against him, saying, “May الله not make your trade profitable.” أبو هريرة 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #2 said, “إذا you see someone buy or sell in the المسجد, then say, ‘May الله not make your trade profitable.’...’"4] " The People of Suffah: Poor الصحابة 4 who lived in the المسجد of الرسول ﷺ of AJlah #8; there they ate and slept. ¥) Bukhaaree related this narration (442). ‘41 Related by At-Tirmidhee (1321), who said, “This الحديث is حسن Ghareeb.”” لأن of the grading of the الحديث, العلماء have differed about its application. Some العلماء, مثل أحمد and Ishaq, have Related Issue: فيما يخص the ruling for buying and selling in rooms or halls that are connected to the المسجد, or halls that are used for الصلاة, the Permanent Council (of العلماء in Saudi Arabia) issued this ruling: “إذا a room is specifically used for الصلاة, and إذا it is connected to the المسجد, لا يجوز to buy or sell in it, nor is it permissible to advertise products inside of it. النبي ﷺ 2% indeed said, ‘إذا you see someone buy or sell in the المسجد, then say: May الله not make your trade profitable...” أما rooms, then we need to elaborate. إذا a room is within the enclosure of a المسجد, then it takes the ruling of a المسجد, and what we said about halls applies to that room. But إذا the room is outside the المسجد’s enclosure, it does not take the ruling of the المسجد, even إذا there is a door that connects it to the المسجد. This tuling is based on the fact that النبي ﷺ's house, in which ‘Aaisha % lived, had a door that was connected to the المسجد, yet his house did not take the same ruling as the المسجد.” Question: In saying, “May الله not make your trade profitable,” should we distinguish between a person who knows the ruling for trading in the المسجد and a person who doesn’t know the ruling? Answer: The plain meaning of the الحديث’s wording indicates that ينبغي لنا say the phrase both to a person who knows the ruling for trading in the المسجد and to a person who doesn't know the ruling. 12) The Prohibition Of Asking Or Making An Announcement In The - المسجد About A Lost Possession الله’s Masjids are built for His الذكر, His glorification, the التلاوة of the Qur’an, and الصلاة; they are not built as ruled that it is Makrooh (disliked) to buy and sell in the المسجد. Other العلماء have ruled that يجوز to buy and sell in the المسجد. And the الحديث is أيضاً related by Ad-Daarimee (1401). الباب - 11 The الآداب Of Attending Masjids 261 places where people go to ask about their lost possessions. أبو هريرة % related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢ said, 56 Grle 635 V YB ae p Lt ES Gee yn “Whoever hears a person ask or make an announcement in the المسجد about a lost possession, should say, ‘May الله not return it to you,' for Masjids were not built for this," لذلك, upon seeing a person who is asking about a lost possession, you should say to him, ‘May الله not return it to you.” 13) Speaking Loudly In The المسجد بينما he was in the المسجد, Ka’ab Ibn Maalik asked Ibn Abee Hadrad to pay him back for a loan. In the ensuing discussion, they raised their voices, to the degree that الرسول ﷺ of الله # heard them, حتى وإن he was in his home. He # went out to them, uncovering the barrier from his apartment. He # then called out, “O Ka’ab.” Ka’ab # said, ‘Here I am, answering your call, O Messenger of الله #%.”” He #& said, “Remove (forgive) a part of this debt of yours,” بعد which he signaled that Ka’ab should forgive Ibn Abee Hadrad % for half of the debt. Ka’ab 4 said, “I have indeed done لذا, O Messenger of الله.” النبي ﷺ # then said to Ibn Abee Hadrad #, “Stand and pay him back,’?) ') The الحديث is related with different wordings by مسلم (568), أحمد (8382, 9161), At-Tirmidhee (1321), Abu Daawood (473), Ibn Maajah (767), and Ad-Daarimee (1401). 2) Related by Bukhaaree (457), مسلم (1558), أحمد (15364), An-Nassaaee (408), Abu Daawood (3595), Ibn Maajah (2429), and Ad-Daarimee (2587). Upon contemplating the two above-mentioned Altadeeth, one might conclude that they are in apparent contradiction with بعضهم بعضاً. الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% did not reproach those who raised their voices in the المسجد; instead, he simply told Ka’ab 4, one of the two who raised their voices, to reduce the debt by one half. النبي ﷺ #% never delayed issuing a ruling بعد its necessary time; here النبي ﷺ ## had the opportunity to reproach them for raising their voices, yet he didn’t do لذا, which indicates that raising one’s voice in the المسجد is not forbidden. “‘عمر’s narration, من ناحية أخرى, indicates that it is Makrooh (disliked) to raise one’s voice in the المسجد. Possessing a noble الأخلاق, ‘عمر . would not have reproached them had he not known some proof from النبي ﷺ #%; ومن ثَم his reproach holds the same weight as a الحديث that is ascribed to النبي ﷺ #8. Perhaps the combined الفهم of the two narrations can be explained by a saying of Maalik (in one of his two narrations): “A distinction must be made between one raising his voice for [imparting] العلم, for [some] الخير [purpose], or for something that is necessary - which is all permissible - and between one raising his voice with clamor, [nonsense], and whatever is similar - which is all forbidden.)
